$schema_doc: Curated registry of mosque slugs on mawaqit.net used as ground-truth fixtures by scripts/fetch-mawaqit.js. Each ACTIVE entry's prayer times are pulled into eval/data/test/mawaqit.json (or train/mawaqit-morocco.json for Moroccan entries) on each fetch run. EXCLUDED entries record slugs that returned data-quality issues (timezone misconfigs, malformed time arrays, stale Ramadan-DST data) so future agents don't re-add them blindly. ICONIC entries are mosques of high scholarly/historical significance whose inclusion is prioritised — populated from canonical references (UNESCO Islamic World Heritage, OIC publications, classical fiqh registries) per the user's scholarly-corpus mandate.
license: Mosque-published times are public broadcast data; this registry is metadata only (slugs, locations, timezone) and is MIT-licensed alongside the rest of fajr.

active.$searchKeyword_reason: Mawaqit search for 'fes' returns French mosques matching 'frais'/'compagnons'; 'fez' disambiguates.
Mawaqit search for 'fes' returns French mosques matching 'frais'/'compagnons'; 'fez' disambiguates.

references: Al Akhawayn University in Ifrane — flagship Moroccan English-medium liberal-arts university (founded 1995)

excluded.reason: Times +60 min off all other Moroccan mosques — likely UTC+2 misconfiguration
Times -60 min off — likely stale Ramadan-DST data (UTC+0)
Times +30 min off — likely stale Ramadan-DST data
Mawaqit returned malformed times (dhuhr 05:54). Mawaqit's Türkiye coverage has systematic data-quality issues; Diyanet's ezanvakti.emushaf.net remains the institutional channel.
Mawaqit returned malformed times (dhuhr 05:54). See ali-qushji entry for context.

iconic_wishlist.$comment: Mosques of high scholarly / historical significance whose inclusion is prioritised. Populated from canonical references per scholarly-corpus mandate.
iconic_wishlist.$mawaqit_search_finding_2026_05_02: An exhaustive sweep tried 30+ canonical mosque names against Mawaqit's search endpoint with multiple keyword variants (city / mosque-name / spelling alternates). Result: 0 of the canonical state-monumental mosques are listed on Mawaqit. The hits surfaced (e.g. azhar-academy-bolton, cordoba-essen-germany, suleymaniye-ludenscheid-germany, masjid-darul-istiqlal-thailand) are DIASPORA mosques NAMED AFTER the canonical ones, not the canonicals themselves. Mawaqit's coverage is structurally community/diaspora — state-monumental mosques are managed by national awqaf apps (IACAD Dulook for UAE, Saudi Hajj Ministry for Haramain, Egyptian GAS for Al-Azhar, Astan Quds Razavi for Imam Reza, etc.). Future iconic-wishlist coverage requires non-Mawaqit channels — see `recommended_channel` per entry.

candidates.name: Masjid al-Haram
candidates.significance: Holiest mosque in Islam (Qiblah). Imam-published times are the institutional reference for Umm al-Qura method.
references: Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah official Imsakiyya
UNESCO World Heritage Tentative List
candidates.recommended_channel: Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah Imsakiyya (no public API; PDF transcription)

candidates.name: Al-Masjid an-Nabawi
candidates.significance: Prophet's Mosque. Second-holiest in Islam.
references: Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah official Imsakiyya
candidates.recommended_channel: Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah Imsakiyya (no public API; PDF transcription)

candidates.name: Al-Aqsa Mosque
candidates.significance: Third holiest mosque in Islam. First Qiblah.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Old City of Jerusalem, 1981)
Jerusalem Awqaf Department published Imsakiyya
candidates.recommended_channel: Jerusalem Awqaf Department (Aladhan custom-method-3 as proxy)

candidates.name: Al-Qarawiyyin
candidates.significance: Founded 859 CE — recognised by UNESCO and Guinness as the oldest continuously operating university and one of the oldest continuously operating mosques in the world.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Medina of Fez, 1981)
Habous Ministry official timetable
candidates.recommended_channel: Habous Ministry (Aladhan custom-method-99 as proxy; mosque-published via Mawaqit captures community calibration)

candidates.name: Hassan II Mosque
candidates.significance: Largest mosque in Morocco; principal Friday mosque of the Habous Ministry.
references: Habous Ministry official Imsakiyya
candidates.recommended_channel: Habous Ministry (Aladhan custom-method-99 as proxy; mosque-published via Mawaqit captures community calibration)

candidates.name: Al-Azhar Mosque
candidates.significance: Founded 970 CE. Seat of Al-Azhar University, the leading institution of Sunni Islamic scholarship.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Cairo, 1979)
Egyptian General Authority of Survey official Imsakiyya
candidates.recommended_channel: Egyptian General Authority of Survey (Aladhan method 5 as proxy; Al-Azhar may publish own Imsakiyya)

candidates.name: Mosque of Muhammad Ali
candidates.significance: Citadel of Saladin (UNESCO Historic Cairo).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Cairo, 1979)
candidates.recommended_channel: Egyptian General Authority of Survey (Aladhan method 5 as proxy; Al-Azhar may publish own Imsakiyya)

candidates.name: Sultan Ahmed (Blue) Mosque
candidates.significance: Iconic Ottoman imperial mosque (1616).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Areas of Istanbul, 1985)
Diyanet İşleri Başkanlığı official ezanvakti
candidates.recommended_channel: Diyanet ezanvakti.emushaf.net (already integrated; covers all Türkiye mosques by zone)

candidates.name: Süleymaniye Mosque
candidates.significance: Mimar Sinan masterwork (1557); seat of Ottoman Sheikh ul-Islam.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Areas of Istanbul, 1985)
candidates.recommended_channel: Diyanet ezanvakti.emushaf.net (already integrated; covers all Türkiye mosques by zone)

candidates.name: Hagia Sophia
candidates.significance: Reverted to mosque 2020.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Areas of Istanbul, 1985)
candidates.recommended_channel: Diyanet ezanvakti.emushaf.net (already integrated; covers all Türkiye mosques by zone)

candidates.name: Selimiye Mosque
candidates.significance: Mimar Sinan's stated masterpiece (1574).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Selimiye Mosque and its Social Complex, 2011)
candidates.recommended_channel: Diyanet ezanvakti.emushaf.net (already integrated; covers all Türkiye mosques by zone)

candidates.name: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
candidates.significance: National grand mosque of the UAE; institutional reference for IACAD method.
references: IACAD (Islamic Affairs and Charitable Activities Department) Dulook DXB app
candidates.recommended_channel: IACAD Dulook DXB app (Sheikh Zayed times via Abu Dhabi zone; floor-stratified for Burj Khalifa)

candidates.name: Faisal Mosque
candidates.significance: National mosque of Pakistan; principal reference for the Karachi 18°/18° calculation convention.
references: University of Islamic Sciences Karachi published timetable
candidates.recommended_channel: Pakistan Auqaf Department (Aladhan method 1 as proxy)

candidates.name: Badshahi Mosque
candidates.significance: Mughal imperial mosque (1673).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Fort and Shalamar Gardens of Lahore, 1981) — adjacent
Punjab Auqaf Department
candidates.recommended_channel: Pakistan Auqaf Department (Aladhan method 1 as proxy)

candidates.name: Masjid al-Negara (National Mosque)
candidates.significance: National mosque of Malaysia; institutional reference for JAKIM 20°/18° + ihtiyati.
references: JAKIM (Department of Islamic Development Malaysia) e-Solat / waktusolat.app
candidates.recommended_channel: JAKIM e-Solat / waktusolat.app (already integrated; Masjid Negara via KL zone)

candidates.name: Istiqlal Mosque
candidates.significance: Largest mosque in Southeast Asia; principal reference for KEMENAG 20°/18° method.
references: KEMENAG (Ministry of Religious Affairs of Indonesia) jadwalsholat.bimasislam.kemenag.go.id
candidates.recommended_channel: KEMENAG jadwalsholat.bimasislam.kemenag.go.id (NOT yet integrated; opportunity)

candidates.name: Masjid Sultan Singapura
candidates.significance: National mosque of Singapore (1928); principal reference for MUIS jadual waktu solat.
references: MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura) muis.gov.sg
Singapore National Heritage Board
candidates.recommended_channel: MUIS muis.gov.sg (NOT yet integrated; opportunity)

candidates.name: Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien Mosque
candidates.significance: National mosque of Brunei.
references: Brunei Ministry of Religious Affairs jadual waktu solat
candidates.recommended_channel: Brunei MoRA jadual waktu solat (NOT yet integrated)

candidates.name: Mosque-Cathedral of Córdoba
candidates.significance: 8th-century Umayyad masterwork (now a Catholic cathedral; structure is a UNESCO site).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Centre of Córdoba, 1984)
candidates.recommended_channel: Cordoba Cathedral is Catholic since 1236 — no Islamic prayer-time publication; UNESCO heritage value only

candidates.name: Great Mosque of Kairouan
candidates.significance: 670 CE; the oldest mosque in Africa and the fourth holiest in Sunni Islam.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Kairouan, 1988)
candidates.recommended_channel: Tunisian Ministère des Affaires Religieuses (Aladhan method 5 as proxy)

candidates.name: Great Mosque of Djenné
candidates.significance: Largest mud-brick mosque in the world; centre of West African Islamic scholarship.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Old Towns of Djenné, 1988)
candidates.recommended_channel: No central awqaf API; Mauritanian / West African community calculation conventions

candidates.name: Great Mosque of Touba
candidates.significance: Mouride brotherhood seat; one of the largest mosques in Africa.
references: Mouride Caliphate official publications
candidates.recommended_channel: Mouride Caliphate publications (Touba) — typically print-only

candidates.name: Great Mosque of Damascus (Umayyad)
candidates.significance: 705 CE; one of the largest and oldest mosques in the world.
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Ancient City of Damascus, 1979)
candidates.recommended_channel: Syrian Ministry of Awqaf (limited public API access)

candidates.name: Imam Reza Shrine (Mashhad)
candidates.significance: Shia 8th Imam shrine; largest mosque complex in the world by area.
references: Astan Quds Razavi (custodial body) published timetable
candidates.recommended_channel: Astan Quds Razavi (Mashhad) for Imam Reza; for Shah Mosque use Aladhan Tehran method

candidates.name: Shah Mosque (Imam Mosque)
candidates.significance: Safavid masterwork (1611–1629).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Meidan Emam, Isfahan, 1979)
candidates.recommended_channel: Astan Quds Razavi (Mashhad) for Imam Reza; for Shah Mosque use Aladhan Tehran method

candidates.name: Bibi Heybat Mosque
candidates.significance: Caucasian Sunni reference mosque.
candidates.recommended_channel: No central institutional API; Aladhan method 13 (Diyanet) as proxy for Sunni mosques

candidates.name: Hazratbal Shrine
candidates.significance: Reliquary mosque; Kashmir's premier shrine.
candidates.recommended_channel: No central awqaf API; Hilal moonsighting Committee India publications

candidates.name: Jama Masjid (Delhi)
candidates.significance: Largest mosque in India (Mughal, 1656).
candidates.recommended_channel: No central awqaf API; Hilal moonsighting Committee India publications

candidates.name: Sultan Hassan Mosque
candidates.significance: Mamluk monumental architecture (1356).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Historic Cairo, 1979)
candidates.recommended_channel: Egyptian General Authority of Survey (Aladhan method 5 as proxy; Al-Azhar may publish own Imsakiyya)

candidates.name: Great Mosque of Samarra
candidates.significance: 9th-century Abbasid; iconic spiral minaret (Malwiya).
references: UNESCO World Heritage (Samarra Archaeological City, 2007)
candidates.recommended_channel: Iraqi Sunni / Shia Awqaf Diwans (limited API access)
